decisions.question: Bootstrap UX model: where does the worktree live, when is the registry entry written, and is the scaffold pre-committed or left dirty for the user?
Does v1 distinguish 'remote exists but has zero commits' (currently 404 indistinguishable from missing/private) from 'wrong slug' via an extra repos/get API call, or punt with a hint?

Scope of adjacent failure-mode classification in v1: refactor error taxonomy with typed sentinels (malformed/auth/404/etc.) for case-specific hints, ship bootstrap path only, or middle ground (string-match hints at the init seam, defer typed refactor)?
Minimal-scaffold derivation details: active vs commented [[sources]]/[groups.<vis>], GitHub-API fallback for visibility, .gitkeep in .niwa/claude/, --from slug as audit comment?
